Ad Text:
About Natrio & Axima

Natrio began over 40 years ago in Brazil and has since grown into a globally recognised supplier of sustainable products and integrated supply chain services. Our capabilities span bulk material handling, freight forwarding, and contract logistics, with warehousing and distribution facilities across the world offering tailored solutions to our customers. We are specialists in manufacturing, mining, agricultural and industrial products including soda ash, limestone, alumina, fertiliser, grain, and cement.

In Australia, our operations are delivered through Natrio and Axima — together providing an end-to-end supply chain solution from bulk material import and handling through to international freight forwarding, customs brokerage, and 3PL warehousing and distribution across Asia Pacific.

About the Role

We are looking for a hands-on Process Automation Engineer to help us eliminate manual work and streamline processes across the entire business. You will be identifying bottlenecks in various departments and building smart, reliable systems to fix them.

Based in Melbourne, you will work with our Digital Engineering team to design and deploy workflows that combine custom code and the most effective automation tools available today.

What You Will Do

Identify, map, and automate repetitive business processes wherever they occur.

Build and maintain flexible automation workflows using the best tools for the job—whether that is custom Python, modern AI orchestrators, or integration platforms.

Develop custom Python scripts for API integrations, data processing, and system connectivity.

Integrate AI models and LLMs into daily business workflows to improve decision-making and data extraction.

Work closely with the rest of the team to brainstorm, troubleshoot, and ensure solutions are practical and genuinely solve the problem.

What We Are Looking For

Strong Python Background: You are comfortable writing clean, maintainable scripts to solve practical problems.

Tool Agnosticism: You aren't tied to one specific platform. You adapt quickly to a fast-changing AI and tech landscape and pick up new tools as needed.

API & Data Integration: Solid understanding of REST APIs, webhooks, and handling various data formats.

Collaborative Team Player: You communicate clearly and enjoy working alongside both technical peers and non-technical stakeholders to understand their challenges and build great solutions together.

Degree in Engineering, IT or similar.  Two or three years’ experience in a fast-paced workplace where you will be able to demonstrate successful automations.

Bonus Points If You Have:

DevOps & Cloud: Familiarity with Google Cloud Platform (GCP), Kubernetes, and structuring isolated cloud environments.

Experience building multi-agent AI systems.

Previous experience automating logistics, supply chain, or enterprise systems.

Why join Natrio

Permanent Full-Time position (4 days from the CBD office and 1 day from home)

Opportunity to work with cutting-edge technologies and innovative products

Be part of a fast-growing company

Join a team built on strong values of customer focus, courage, ownership, support, integrity and respect

Competitive salary and superannuation package

Career growth opportunities

Access to our Employee Assistance Program

To Apply

Applicants must have the right to live and work in Australia

Apply directly through Seek

 We encourage you to apply today and become part of a team where reliability is above all else.

We’re partnering with a leading Australian brand undergoing a significant transformation across Data & AI. This program is focused on rethinking how contract data is understood and how procurement experiences are designed. 
  
This is not a strategy or architecture-only role. This is about building real, working products. You’ll sit inside a high-impact transformation team, developing internal POCs that layer on top of established enterprise platforms - leveraging modern AI tooling, data platforms, and cloud infrastructure. The work spans contract ingestion, document intelligence, AI agents, and user-facing tools. 
  
We’re looking for someone who can move seamlessly from concept to code - comfortable whiteboarding with architects one moment, then building and deploying working solutions the next. 

Location: Sydney (Hybrid | 3 days/week in office)
Length: 12 months (possible extensions)
Rate: $1000-$1100 + GST

What You’ll Do

Build and iterate end-to-end AI-powered POCs — from ingestion and extraction through to analysis and front-end experience
Develop solutions across structured and unstructured data, including document processing and intelligent workflows
Design and implement AI-driven components (LLMs, agents, automation workflows) embedded in real applications
Rapidly prototype, test, and refine solutions, demonstrating progress to business and technical stakeholders
Collaborate with enterprise architecture and platform teams to ensure alignment with broader technology strategy
Contribute to, and challenge technical design decisions early in the lifecycle
Translate ambiguous problem spaces into clear, executable build plans
Produce clear documentation to enable handover to internal or scaled delivery teams for productionisation
What You’ll Bring

Proven experience building and deploying AI-enabled applications, not just experimenting in notebooks
Hands-on delivery experience with AWS Bedrock in a real-world setting
Strong capability working across data pipelines.
Ability to develop functional front-end interfaces (React) for internal tools and POCs
Experience integrating LLMs, document intelligence, and/or agent-based systems into usable applications
Strong communication skills and able to engage both non-technical stakeholders and enterprise architecture teams
A self-directed mindset - i.e. comfortable operating in ambiguity and defining your own path to delivery
A bias toward action - you build, test, and iterate quickly
Experience with modern data and AI platforms (e.g. Snowflake, Databricks, Fabric, Redshift) is highly regarded
If you have any questions about this role or others we have available you can email our team directly via jack@thedrivegroup.com.au 
   
For The Latest Jobs, Tech News or to 'Introduce A Friend' for a $1000 Referral Fee - Follow Us On Linkedin:
   
https://www.linkedin.com/company/thedrivegroup
   
TheDriveGroup is 100% committed to improving meaningful diversity in the technology industry.
   
We partner with clients who embrace diversity and seek candidates across all backgrounds, abilities, genders, sexualities, cultures, and faiths.
   
If you have the right skills and attitude we would love to hear from you!

Company Overview

UGL is a diversified services company delivering critical assets and essential services that sustain and enhance our living environment. We provide a broad range of services and whole-of-life solutions for various industries, utilizing sustainable and innovative technologies.

UGL Regional Linx (UGLRL) is a subsidiary of UGL, headquartered in Orange, NSW, with regional depots throughout the state. We focus on creating job opportunities in regional and rural communities, with over 90% of our workforce based in regional NSW. We manage the CRN – Country Regional Network, ensuring safe transportation of people and goods across NSW.

Job Purpose
The Asset Engineer and Configuration Manager will oversee the management, monitoring, reporting of the configuration management for the Operation and Maintenance (O&M) on Country Regional Network (CRN) contract. The position will support the AM Framework and is responsible for maintaining configuration management of all Asset Information Systems (AIS) such as Infor and support the business in timely reporting from the systems as required for UGL Regional Linx management team.
The position will be required to provide develop, maintain and delivery AIS training across the UGLRL business as required.  The role may include the management of a small internal team and associated contractors.

Key Responsibilities
Contribute to the AM framework consistent with ISO 55001; establish optimal long-term asset strategies
Maintaining and updating asset data within various systems, including Asset Management Information Systems (HxGN and GIS) across the asset life
Ensuring the accuracy, completeness, and relevance of asset data through audits, validation, and correction processes.
Responsible for maintaining the Configuration Management Plan, forms and checklists.
Updating and configuring asset management information systems to meet organizational needs and external requirements.
Manages and monitors the Configuration Management Change process end to end for UGLRL for existing assets and any enhancement projects.
Manages the coordination of the Configuration Control Board (CCB) including all activities associated with the CCB
Provides system assurance at various asset life cycle gates as required. 
Ensuring that asset data management practices comply with internal and external regulations and standards.
Identifying opportunities to improve data management processes and systems
Contribute and revise the Asset Management Plan (50AMP, 10MSP, 3MWP and Asset Services Plan) in accordance with the O&M Deed. 
Supports the MPM Planning of the 3MWP with the Asset Engineer
Ensure compliance with relevant regulatory, legislative, and international standards for AEO and Rail Safety Accreditation
Maintain and Monitor all AIS including the EAM Infor system and ensure asset records are maintained across the asset life cycle.

Qualifications
Degree or Diploma in an Engineering or related discipline.
5+ years business operational experience within an operations and maintenance /resources industries.
Proficiency in using data management systems, including GIS and Enterprise Asset Management Systems (EAMs) in an O&M environment.
Expertise in GIS software such as ArcGIS, QGIS, or other relevant platforms.
Experience in managing and maintaining spatial databases.
Sound understanding of Asset Management and Systems Assurance standards.

About Us
UGL is renowned for providing comprehensive engineering, construction, and maintenance services across various sectors, including rail and transport, communications and technology systems, oil and gas, power, renewables, water, and defense. We partner with some of the world’s largest companies and government agencies.

Diversity
We embrace diversity in all its forms—backgrounds, ideas, work styles, and perspectives. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we strongly encourage applications from women, individuals with disabilities, Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islander people, mature-aged workers, and those from culturally diverse backgrounds.

Our selection process includes relevant screening checks such as national criminal history and pre-employment medical assessments, including drug and alcohol screening.

Teams

For more information contact Mitchell on 0425 269 780.

Company: One Solution Mobile & Data Pty Ltd
Category: Help Desk & IT Support (Information & Communication Technology)
Ad Text:
ABOUT 1SOLUTION

1Solution is an Adelaide-based ICT managed services provider and Telstra Platinum Partner with a team of 60+ professionals. We deliver enterprise-grade technology solutions to government, enterprise, and SMB clients across South Australia and nationally. Our portfolio spans managed services, mobility, connectivity, unified communications, and AI-powered software through our 1AI division.

POSITION OVERVIEW

The MDM Engineer is a specialist technical role responsible for the design, deployment, management, and optimisation of Mobile Device Management environments across 1Solution's client base. This role sits at the intersection of mobility engineering, endpoint security, and network access control, working across concurrent multi-platform MDM environments for government and enterprise clients.

This is a high-impact role with direct exposure to complex government mobility programmes. You will be expected to operate independently, manage competing priorities across multiple client environments, and contribute to internal knowledge systems and documentation standards.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

MDM Platform Administration

Deploy, configure, and maintain Microsoft Intune and VMware Workspace ONE (UEM) environments for managed clients.
Manage concurrent MDM environments across multiple client tenants, ensuring consistency across enrolment, policy, and compliance configurations.
Administer device enrolment, policy management, app deployment, and compliance frameworks across iOS, Android, and Windows endpoints.
Build and maintain configuration profiles, compliance policies, and automated remediation workflows.

Network Access & Security Integration

Implement network access controls and conditional access policies aligned to client security policy and government frameworks.
Support identity-based access through Azure AD / Entra ID Conditional Access policies.
Collaborate with network and security teams to resolve access control issues impacting device connectivity.

Project Delivery & Governance

Lead and support MDM migration and deployment projects from scoping through to completion.
Produce and maintain accurate technical documentation, including build guides, runbooks, and change records.
Engage directly with client stakeholders, providing clear technical communication and escalation management.
Contribute to governance documentation for complex multi-environment deployments, including risk registers and environment health reports.

Managed Services Support

Provide Tier 2/3 escalation support for MDM-related service desk tickets and client escalations.
Participate in incident management processes, ensuring timely resolution and root cause documentation.
Proactively monitor environment health, identifying and resolving issues before they impact end users.
Support the onboarding of new MDM-managed clients, including environment design and initial configuration. 

PLATFORM / TECHNOLOGY & LEVEL REQUIRED

Microsoft Intune—Advanced
VMware Workspace ONE (UEM)—Advanced
Azure AD / Entra ID— Intermediate-Advanced
Microsoft 365 / Conditional Access —Intermediate
iOS / iPadOS / Android Enterprise—Advanced
Windows 10/11 Endpoint Management—Advanced
PowerShell / Automation Scripting—Intermediate

QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE

Essential

Minimum 3 years' experience in an MDM, endpoint, or mobility engineering role.
Hands-on experience with Microsoft Intune and/or VMware Workspace ONE in a managed services or enterprise environment.
Demonstrated ability to manage multiple client environments simultaneously, with strong attention to detail.
Experience working with government or enterprise clients with complex security and compliance requirements.
Strong written communication skills — able to produce clear technical documentation and client-facing reports.

Desirable

Microsoft certifications — MD-102 (Endpoint Administrator) or equivalent.
VMware Workspace ONE / Omnissa certifications.
Familiarity with PROTECTED or ISM-aligned security frameworks in a government context.
Scripting experience (PowerShell, Bash, or similar) for MDM automation and reporting.
Exposure to Telstra mobility platforms and enterprise SIM/connectivity management.

WHAT WE OFFER

A high-performing, collaborative team with deep government and enterprise client relationships.
Exposure to complex, multi-platform mobility programmes across South Australia's largest agencies.
Ongoing access to vendor training, certifications, and professional development support.
Involvement in 1AI innovation initiatives including AI-assisted tooling and automation.
A leadership team invested in your growth and a culture that values technical depth and accountability.

1Solution is committed to building diverse, high-performing teams. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds. If you have strong MDM platform expertise and are looking for a role where your work directly shapes outcomes for major government clients, we want to hear from you. If you'd like to learn more about us, visit our website at https://onesolution.net.au/.

To apply, please send a PDF copy of your CV and a brief cover letter through SEEK.

Ad Text:

Equality & Opportunity for All

Representing 165 nationalities across the globe, we pride ourselves on being an equal opportunity employer, committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees regardless of race, religion, sex, color, age, national origin, pregnancy, sexual orientation, physical disability or genetic information, military or veteran status, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people or any other protected classification, in accordance with federal, state, and/or local law.




Note: Please follow the link to apply to the role with your recent resume. If you need more details, feel free to contact Garima on garima.si@hcltech.com.




Candidate Data Privacy Notice | HCL Technologies

Company description:



Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:



DevOps Engineer - AI | Technology & GenAI - Productionisation & Automation | Melbourne

Be at the forefront of our AI journey

Choice and flexibility on where, when and how you work

Learn from the best in the business 

What will your typical day look like?

As a DevOps Engineer, you'll be at the heart of Deloitte's ClearTrack Generative AI & Digital Assets platform, helping design, build and run highly secure, scalable GenAI infrastructure that powers real-world outcomes. You'll work closely with AI engineers, developers and architects to keep platforms fast, resilient and production-ready — while continuously evolving our stack to stay ahead of emerging AI technologies.

In this role, you will:

Design, implement and operate the central GenAI & Assets platform, built on Kubernetes

Engineer reliable, secure cloud infrastructure across AWS and/or Azure

Monitor performance, availability and cost, optimising systems for scale and speed

Troubleshoot complex system, software and networking issues

Own system security, patching, updates and resilience

Drive Infrastructure as Code, CI/CD and DevSecOps best practice

Ensure robust backup, disaster recovery and platform documentation

Partner with IT, security and engineering teams to integrate new AI capabilities

About the team

The National Generative AI and Assets team sits within Deloitte's Strategy, Transformation & Innovation office — a group shaping the future of how the firm delivers value to its largest and most complex clients.

We bring together Design, AI and Software Engineering to build enterprise-grade GenAI platforms and digital products that improve productivity, decision-making and business performance. Our work moves quickly from idea to production, combining cutting-edge technology with strong governance and security.

If you're excited by scaling AI responsibly in enterprise environments, this is where it happens.

What success looks like:

High availability, low-latency GenAI platforms running in production
Secure, compliant systems with minimal downtime
Well-documented, automated infrastructure and deployment pipelines
Continuous improvements to performance, reliability and developer experience
On-time delivery of platform enhancements and upgrades

Enough about us — let's talk about you

You bring strong systems engineering fundamentals plus hands-on GenAI platform experience.

You'll likely have:

A minimum of 3-5 years professional experience as a Systems Engineer, preferably with a focus on Generative AI
Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field is preferrable
Experience with platform and software development, release management in a mature managed services environment

Technical experience we're looking for:

Core engineering

AWS and/or Azure cloud platforms

Linux/UNIX systems administration

Networking, databases, messaging and security fundamentals

Infrastructure as Code (Terraform)

CI/CD and DevSecOps tools (GitHub, ADO, ArgoCD)

Identity and security protocols (OAuth, SAML, OpenID Connect)

Generative AI & platform experience is desirable:

Designing, building and operating AI chatbots and conversational AI systems

Hands-on experience with LLM frameworks (LangChain, Haystack, OpenAI / Azure OpenAI)

Building RAG-based solutions (document ingestion, embeddings, semantic search)

Experience with agentic and automation frameworks (n8n, LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI)

Strong understanding of AI tooling: vector databases, prompt management, model evaluation

Integrating AI systems with enterprise platforms, APIs and workflows

Solid software engineering skills (Python and/or JavaScript) in production environments

Knowledge of AI governance, security and responsible AI in enterprise settings

Why Deloitte?  

At Deloitte, we focus our energy on interesting and impactful work. We're always learning, innovating and setting the standard; making a positive difference to our clients and our society. We put coaching at the heart of what we do, helping our people grow their careers in any direction - whether it be up, moving into something new, or even moving across the world.   
We embrace diversity, equity and inclusion. We have a diverse collection of people from different backgrounds, with different experiences, gender identities, abilities and thinking styles. What binds us together is a shared commitment to value everyone's perspective and to cultivate inclusion; so that our work environment is a safe space we can all belong.  
We support flexibility and choice. We encourage you to find the right balance between connecting in person with your clients and teams and meeting your own personal needs.
We help you live and work well. To support your personal and professional life, we offer a range of perks and benefits, including retail discounts, wellbeing leave, paid volunteering days, twelve flexible working options, market-leading parental leave and return to work support package.  

Next Steps

Apply now, we'd love to hear from you!

#LI-Hybrid

Level 3 Systems Engineer - Sydney


About the Role
We are partnering with a growing managed services provider to hire a Level 3 Systems Engineer to join their high-performing Assist Team.


This role will see you operating as a senior escalation point across complex infrastructure and security environments, while also playing a key role in technical leadership, automation, and continuous improvement initiatives.


You'll work across a broad range of Microsoft, virtualisation, and network technologies, supporting enterprise clients while mentoring junior engineers and driving best practice across the team.


Key Responsibilities

Provide Level 3 support across systems, infrastructure, and network environments
Troubleshoot and resolve complex incidents across Microsoft technologies (Windows Server, M365, Exchange, SharePoint, SQL)
Support and manage virtualisation platforms (VMware, Hyper-V)
Maintain and support Citrix environments (Virtual Apps, Desktops, Netscaler, WEM)
Deliver network-level support across WAN/LAN, firewalls, routing, and connectivity
Act as a senior escalation point for L1/L2 engineers and assist with critical incident resolution
Communicate effectively with clients, providing updates, managing expectations, and ensuring service quality
Key Skills & Experience
Strong experience in a Level 3 Systems / Infrastructure Engineer role
Deep expertise across Microsoft environments (Windows Server, M365, Azure)
Experience with virtualisation (VMware, Hyper-V) and Citrix technologies
Strong networking knowledge (WAN/LAN, firewalls, routing, security)
Experience with endpoint management (Intune, Autopilot)
Scripting/automation experience (PowerShell highly desirable)
Exposure to security frameworks and tools (Defender, Secure Score, etc.)
Proven experience in a managed services or client-facing environment
Certifications (Desirable)
Microsoft Certifications (AZ-104, MS-500, M365 Expert)
Security certifications (Security+, CISSP, CISM, etc.)
Other relevant infrastructure or cloud certifications
Ideal Candidate Profile
Strong technical depth with a proactive and solutions-focused mindset
Experience working in MSP or multi-client environments
Comfortable mentoring others and leading technical initiatives
Strong communication skills with the ability to engage with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Commercially aware with a focus on delivering high-quality outcomes

Randstad is currently working with a sovereign defence manufacturer of high-tech defence systems that are integrated onto various defence platforms operating in mission critical environments. They are looking for an experienced ILS Engineer to work on a project to support the Australian Defence force in enhancing their surveillance, air battlespace management and air defence systems.

This is a permanent, full-time opportunity based in Adelaide.

About the Role

Performing Supportability Analysis; including Reliability, Availability and Maintainability, Failure Mode Effects and Criticality Analysis, LCC modelling, Level of Repair Analysis, Maintenance Task Analysis and Sparing Analysis, during the development of products/processes to optimise supportability during the In-Service phase of the product lifecycle.
Defining and coordinating the required resources for the efficient running of ILS functions.
Development of ILS deliverables in accordance with project requirements (typically ASDEFCON or derivative).
Participate in project and technical review meetings.
Implementing ILS policies and strategies.

About you

Must possess an NV1 or Baseline Security Clearance.
At least 5 years experience within defence/defence industry acquisition projects performing LSA, coupled with a tertiary degree in Engineering or significant relevant technical experience.
Ability to work to tight deadlines with little supervision.
Ability to use oral and written communication skills to communicate effectively with
supervisors, co-workers and customers.
Ability to prepare concise and detailed written information.
Ability to interpret information of a complex technical nature.
Ability to solve problems and make decisions effectively and efficiently.
Strong analytical skills.

Knowledge of Technical Publication Standards as used by defence.
In depth knowledge of logistics theory and techniques, including the following:
Reliability, Availability and Maintainability analysis
Failure Mode Effects and Criticality Analysis (FMECA)
